component.name: Wayfile
component.summary: Lightweight Qt6/QML file manager for Wayland

description.p: Wayfile is a fast, keyboard-driven file manager built with Qt6 and QML. It runs on any Wayland desktop session and feels at home on tiling compositors.

Wayfile is a comprehensive overhaul of HyprFM by Soyeb Pervez Jim, rebuilt around the obsidian and gold Bifrost design system with a hybrid folders-and-files view, five live accent presets, and a unified sidebar.
Features:

ul.li: Grid, detailed and Miller column views with image and video thumbnails
Full-screen quick preview overlay for images, video, PDFs, audio and text
Tabs with per-tab history and a split pane view for working in two locations at once
Keyboard-first navigation with vim-friendly bindings, type-ahead jump and customizable shortcuts
fd-powered search with glob and full-path matching
Async copy and move via rsync with live progress, speed, ETA and pause/resume
Bulk rename with regular expressions, drag and drop, undo and redo
XDG-compliant trash with restore, archive compress and extract, Open With dialog
Removable device mounting via UDisks2 and remote filesystem browsing (sftp, smb, dav) via gvfs
Git status overlays for modified, staged, untracked and conflicted files
TOML themes with live reload, configurable corner radius, fonts and animations

screenshot.caption: Hybrid view — folder grid over a sortable file list
Grid view with thin-frame, type-coloured file icons
Miller column view with live preview and metadata
Two tabs merged into a split supertab

description.p: First stable release of Wayfile — a Qt6/QML file manager with the obsidian-and-gold Bifrost design system.

ul.li: Hybrid default view: a folder grid over a sortable file list with one shared selection
Thin-frame, type-coloured file icons and accent-coloured folders, with a soft gold bloom on hover and selection
Five live accent presets — Bifrost, Aurora, Nebula, Ember, and Verdant — derived from a single accent on a shared obsidian base
A Colours settings page with a preset picker, token-by-token editing, and save-as-named-theme
Unified sidebar: Favorites with colour tags, an expandable Places tree, devices with capacity meters, and network locations
Merged tabs into split supertabs, Miller columns, quick preview, async file operations, and git status
An Inter, Cormorant Garamond, and JetBrains Mono type stack, and a new compass-folder logo

description.p: Distinct USB icon for removable media, sidebar partitions sorted with removables on top, and settings panel reworked as a floating window.
Adds missing-dependencies dialog, NTFS mount error messages, and a Wayland-only session guard.
Bump Quill submodule so the AppImage/Flatpak builds can load SettingsPanel.
Font preview, keyboard/UX fixes, animation polish, and release automation.
Fix clipboard image paste to use the current clipboard contents and bust thumbnail caches when pasted images reuse an existing file name.
Add a keyboard shortcuts dialog, path bar autocomplete, and paste-pending visual indicators.
Add animations, drag state tracking, and cut-pending indicators.
Smooth theme transitions in settings panel.
Add native KWin blur support, remove minimize/maximize buttons from CSD toolbar, and fix AppImage QML loading.
Install QML module on disk, enable cachegen for faster startup, and gate releases on a QML smoke test to catch broken bundles before they ship.
CI fix: add a terminating newline when writing the Flatpak deploy SSH key to disk so OpenSSH can parse it. Re-release of v0.4.12 / v0.4.13 with the same code changes.
CI fix: configure gpg-agent for loopback pinentry so the release workflow can sign the Flatpak ostree repo inside GitHub Actions, which has no TTY. Re-release of v0.4.12 with the same code changes.
Flatpak sandbox compatibility and UI polish:

ul.li: Git status overlays now work inside the Flatpak build: the git CLI is invoked on the host via flatpak-spawn when running sandboxed.
The default theme respects the system light / dark color scheme on first run.
UI spacing, fonts, control sizes, title bar, and toolbar row height scale with the system font point size.
Window maximized and fullscreen states are now saved and restored across restarts.

description.p: Add a full settings panel with light and dark theme switching, live transparency and animation controls, font and icon-theme selection, editable keyboard shortcuts, and remote-connect entry points. This release also improves font fallback behavior in sandboxed builds so text renders cleanly when preferred fonts are unavailable.

Fix the AppImage on Wayland sessions: build QtWayland from source in CI so the wayland-graphics-integration-client plugins (which aqtinstall's base Qt omits) are present for linuxdeploy to bundle. Without this, Qt aborted on startup with "Available client buffer integrations: QList()".

Flathub submission polish: install the license file at the Flatpak-standard location, pin bundled git sources to immutable commit SHAs, drop unused DBus name claims and redundant filesystem permissions, and reference screenshots by tag instead of branch.

Documentation polish: correct the feature list in the AppStream metadata to match the three views Wayfile actually ships (grid, detailed and Miller columns) and expand it to mention git status overlays, remote access, quick preview and archive support.
Major release adding Flatpak support and reworking several backend pieces for sandbox compatibility.

ul.li: New Flathub-ready Flatpak build targeting the KDE 6.9 runtime.
Mount and unmount now talk to UDisks2 directly over DBus (no udisksctl dependency), which also makes the feature work inside sandboxes.
File icons and thumbnail classification now use the system MIME database, so ambiguous extensions like .ts (TypeScript vs MPEG-TS) and modern image formats like AVIF/HEIC are recognized correctly.
Open With, trash operations, and opening files in host apps all work correctly inside a Flatpak by routing through flatpak-spawn --host.
Sidebar fill bar for the root partition now reports the real host usage inside a Flatpak.
Recents, Search and Trash views no longer fail to render delegates when the model lacks git status roles.

description.p: Bundle the Qt Wayland platform plugin in the AppImage and add qt6-wayland as an explicit dependency in the .deb so the window renders correctly on Ubuntu's GNOME Wayland session.
Detect the fdfind binary on Debian/Ubuntu (where the fd-find package ships fd as fdfind to avoid a name clash).
Initial Flathub-targeted release.
